summary: Matched and improved several camera module functions in src/melee/cm, replacing placeholder declarations with C implementations and tightening camera-related prototypes. The main work landed in camera.c, adding implementations for Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D85C, and Camera_80030E44, plus stack/local-order tweaks in Camera_8002FEEC and formatting cleanup. The PR also refined camera.h signatures to use concrete argument and return types. Review feedback focused on declaration ownership: a non-static camera debug global should not be exposed from camera.static.h; if outside access is required, move it to camera.h instead.
what_changed: Added #include "gr/grlib.h" in camera.c to support newly matched Camera_80030E44 logic calling grLib_801C9CEC.

Implemented Camera_8002CB0C(CameraBounds*), handling pause-camera input: reads CameraInputs via Camera_8002B694, uses 64-bit button-mask checks, applies stick deadzone thresholds, cycles camera target slot x2C4 with Camera_8002BA00, resolves player/stage subjects, resets pause eye/up vectors, updates pause_eye_distance, and dispatches zoom/pan helpers Camera_8002BAA8 and Camera_8002BD88.

Implemented Camera_8002CDDC(void* unused), including transform setup, target-slot validation loops, subject validity checks, success path through Camera_8002C5B4(&cam->x2D0), and fallback paths that update both transform and transform_copy with bounds calculations and ground/camera checks.

Implemented Camera_8002D85C(void* unused), including subject-based camera tracking, fov smoothing, pitch/yaw clamping using Stage_GetCamAngleRadians* helpers, target position calculation with sinf/cosf/tanf, fallback transform processing, bounds smoothing, and rolling bounds offset averaging.

Implemented Camera_80030E44(enum_t arg0, Vec3* arg1), setting camera object/type state, optionally initializing a ground object through grLib_801C9CEC, and filling available _B0 camera entries with either the supplied Vec3 or zero vector.
Adjusted Camera_8002FEEC local variable order and PAD_STACK size, likely to improve stack layout/matching evidence.
Updated camera.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to stronger signatures for Camera_8002C5B4, Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D318, Camera_8002D85C, and Camera_8002E234.
camera.static.h briefly changed cm_80453004 from static to non-static, but review requested removing that exposure or moving any needed external declaration to camera.h.
smart_moves: Used concrete CameraBounds*, CameraTransformState*, CmSubject*, HSD_GObj*, Vec3, and CameraUnkGlobals locals rather than leaving broad unknown signatures, improving both matching readability and future type propagation.
Preserved decompiler/control-flow shape with explicit goto labels such as loop_top, loop_body, fallback_path, check_done, and fallback where structured C would likely perturb codegen.
Used pointer locals like slot_ptr, pitch_ptr, yaw_ptr, base_ptr, coeff_ptr, smooth_ptr, and transform_copy to model original register/address reuse and stack-sensitive code generation.
Matched input bit tests using union-style 64-bit field access, e.g. inputs.x18._u64 & 0x20ULL and inputs.x10._u64 & 0x400ULL, instead of assuming narrower button fields.
Kept threshold comparisons and absolute-value handling explicit for stick deadzones and z-distance checks, which helps preserve floating-point branch/codegen behavior.
Updated headers in parallel with implementations so newly matched functions expose useful prototypes instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders.
Applied formatting cleanup after review, including clang-format spacing in a function-pointer cast.
decomp_lessons: For camera matching, expect heavy coupling between camera.c and player, stage, ground, ftlib, and grlib helpers; include dependencies should be introduced only when the matched function needs them.
Pause/debug camera routines often use sentinel slot values 0xA and 0xB/10 and 11 differently from normal player slots; preserve those checks explicitly when matching target-selection loops.
When a camera routine has both live transform and transform_copy paths, duplicate the same bounds/fov/ground-check sequence rather than over-abstracting it if the goal is matching.
Small stack details matter: local declaration order and PAD_STACK adjustments in Camera_8002FEEC were part of the matching cleanup evidence.
Header improvements are part of decomp progress: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with precise return/argument types documents verified ABI knowledge and helps later functions compile cleanly.

Globals in *.static.h should remain file-local unless there is clear external use; if external access is required, declare through the public module header instead of silently dropping static in the static header.
naming_conventions: Camera functions retain address-based names in the form Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D85C, Camera_80030E44.
Camera globals retain cm_ address names such as cm_80452C68, cm_80453004, and cm_803BCCA0 until better semantic names are proven.
Unknown or partially understood struct fields remain offset-style names such as x2C4, x2C5, x308, x314, x2D0, x2BC, x2C0, x2B8, x2B4, x2B0, x40, x44, x64, x68, x6C, and x70.

Local names in matched code use descriptive-but-low-risk terms such as bounds, transform, transform_copy, slot_ptr, pos_ptr, subject, stick_x, stick_y, zoom_dir, pitch_ptr, yaw_ptr, tgt_fov_ptr, and smooth_ptr.
Unused arguments are explicitly named unused in camera.h prototypes for Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D318, and Camera_8002D85C.
Typed camera structs use names already established in the module: CameraBounds, CameraInputs, CameraTransformState, CameraUnkGlobals, CmSubject, and CameraDebugMode.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Retained goto-based loops and labels to mirror original control flow, especially in slot cycling and fallback paths.
Used explicit s8 casts and s8* slot pointers for camera slot fields to match signed-byte accesses around camera->x2C4 and camera->x2C5.
Used 64-bit masks with ULL constants for input fields, suggesting the original codegen depended on paired/64-bit access rather than separate 32-bit checks.
Kept branch patterns like if (!(x_val == 0.0f && y_val == 0.0f)) and separate if/else absolute-value blocks instead of simplifying them, likely to preserve compiler output.
Used temporary pointer locals to globals and fields to influence register allocation and load/store order.
Maintained explicit duplicate fallback sequences for transform and transform_copy rather than factoring them into helpers, which would not match original assembly.
Adjusted stack padding from PAD_STACK(8) to PAD_STACK(12) and reordered Vec3/local declarations in Camera_8002FEEC for layout-sensitive matching.
review_feedback: Reviewer ribbanya noted that if cm_80453004 needed access outside camera.c, its declaration should be moved to camera.h rather than made non-static in camera.static.h.
Reviewer supplied an empty suggestion on the camera.static.h hunk, effectively requesting removal of the non-static exposure/change.
PR discussion requested running clang-format, with suggested command clang-format -i -- src/**/**.{c,h}; author acknowledged precommit did not run and said they would fix it.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D85C, or Camera_80030E44 to see whether these nonmatching improvements became fully matched or renamed.
Search for cm_80453004 declarations after PR 2071 to confirm whether it stayed static or moved to camera.h in a later cleanup.
Search for other camera PRs using CameraInputs x10._u64 or x18._u64 to reuse the 64-bit input-mask tactic.
Investigate whether CameraUnkGlobals fields x40/x44/x64/x68/x6C/x70 received semantic names in later struct/type cleanup.
Compare later camera.h prototypes for remaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS around Camera_8002C1A8, Camera_8002E490, and related camera functions.
